title: Pluto's Icy Secrets Revealed
story: Pluto, the distant dwarf planet, has been hiding some chilly surprises! On March 29, 2022, scientists announced an exciting discovery from NASA's New Horizons mission. After studying images from the spacecraft's 2015 flyby, they found evidence of ice volcanoes on Pluto's surface. These aren't your typical fiery volcanoes - they spew out icy lava instead! This frosty phenomenon, called cryovolcanism, shows that Pluto is more active than we once thought.
activity_question: What type of volcanoes did scientists discover on Pluto?
follow_up_question: What do you think icy lava might look like as it flows on Pluto's surface?

title: Clay Warriors Unearthed: An Emperor's Secret Army
story: Shovels struck something hard in a field near Xi'an, China. Farmers had stumbled upon an incredible secret: thousands of life-sized clay soldiers buried underground! This amazing discovery in 1974 revealed the Terracotta Army, created over 2,000 years ago. On March 29 that year, the world learned about 8,000 detailed warrior statues. They were made to protect China's first emperor, Qin Shi Huang, in the afterlife.

activity_answer: Protect China's first emperor in the afterlife
follow_up_question: Why do you think the emperor wanted an army to protect him after death?

title: The Day Niagara Falls Went Silent
story: Imagine the roar of Niagara Falls suddenly hushed! In 1848, on March 29, the mighty waterfall stopped flowing for an astonishing 30 hours. A massive ice jam in the river upstream blocked the water's path, creating an eerie silence where thunderous cascades once poured. Locals were amazed to see the bare rock face exposed. Some even ventured out onto the dry riverbed, collecting artifacts and marveling at this once-in-a-lifetime sight.
activity_question: What caused Niagara Falls to stop flowing?
activity_choices: An ice jam in the river
activity_answer: An ice jam in the river
follow_up_question: How do you think people reacted when they saw Niagara Falls without water?

title: Giant Ship Unstuck: Suez Canal Flows Again
story: A colossal container ship called Ever Given got wedged sideways in the Suez Canal, bringing global trade to a standstill. For nearly a week, the megaship blocked this crucial waterway. On March 29, 2021, after days of intense efforts, salvage teams finally freed the massive vessel. Hundreds of waiting ships cheered as they could finally continue their journeys through the reopened canal.
activity_question: What was the name of the giant ship that got stuck in the Suez Canal?
bonus_fact: The Suez Canal is a man-made waterway that connects the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea, allowing ships to avoid a long trip around Africa.

title: Speedy Space Trip Sets New Record
story: Zoom! On March 29, 2013, a spacecraft named Soyuz TMA-08M blasted off on a super-fast journey. It raced through space, reaching the International Space Station in just 6 hours. This quick trip set a new record for the shortest time between launch and docking with the space station. Before this, spacecraft took about two days to make the same trip!

title: Antarctic Ocean Currents in Danger
story: Scientists sounded the alarm on March 29, 2023. Deep ocean currents near Antarctica could shrink by nearly half by 2050. These currents are like underwater highways, carrying important nutrients northward. If they slow down, it could speed up ice melting and make the world's oceans even warmer. Researchers are working hard to understand and protect these vital ocean flows.

activity_choices: It could speed up ice melting
It could make penguins swim faster
activity_answer: It could speed up ice melting
follow_up_question: Why are ocean currents important for the health of our planet?

story: Cheers erupted at Madison Square Garden as the Boston Bruins clinched their first-ever Stanley Cup championship. On March 29, 1929, they defeated the New York Rangers 2-1, completing a thrilling two-game sweep of the finals. Hockey fans watched in awe as the Bruins skated into history, etching their names onto the most coveted trophy in professional ice hockey.
activity_question: Which team did the Boston Bruins defeat to win their first Stanley Cup?
bonus_fact: The Stanley Cup is the oldest trophy in North American professional sports.

title: A Grand Tribute to Honest Abe
story: Honoring a great leader took a big step forward on March 29. In 1867, the US Congress gave the green light to build the Lincoln Memorial. This massive monument would celebrate Abraham Lincoln, the president who guided America through the Civil War. Though it would take many more years to complete, this decision set the stage for creating one of Washington D.C.'s most famous landmarks.
activity_question: What did Congress approve building in 1867?
follow_up_question: Why do you think the Lincoln Memorial is such an important landmark for Americans?

title: Cracking the Mayan Code
story: A brilliant linguist named Yuri Knorosov made a huge breakthrough in 1955. On March 29, he defended his master's thesis in Moscow, explaining a clever new way to read ancient Mayan writing. Knorosov's method used sounds to unlock the secrets of these mysterious symbols. His work opened up a whole new world of understanding about the Maya civilization.
activity_question: What did Yuri Knorosov develop to understand Mayan writing?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important to be able to read ancient writing systems?

title: Cricket Clash: Delhi's Ranji Trophy Triumph
story: Bats swung and wickets fell as Delhi's cricket team smashed their way to victory! In 1979, on March 29, Delhi faced off against Karnataka in the thrilling final of the Ranji Trophy. The match was a showcase of skill and determination. Delhi's players outperformed their opponents, securing an impressive win by nearly four hundred runs. This victory etched Delhi's name in cricket history as Ranji Trophy champions.
activity_question: Which team did Delhi defeat to win the Ranji Trophy?
